title: scorodophloeus fischeri
intro: Scorodophloeus fischeri, also known as the Red Stinkwood or Redwood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Scorodophloeus fischeri is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
